candidate_sources_rejected: older adult-sinusitis summaries without exact opened statements
headache guidance as complete sinus coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Only the opened current DHA document was mapped.
Headache guidance does not establish rhinosinusitis.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for acute-rhinosinusitis-compatible symptoms; not exact for every chronic sinus, headache, dental, tumour, or facial-pain presentation.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person nasal, sinus, dental, or ophthalmic examination.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 document, due for revision 2029-02-21, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA acute-rhinosinusitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped sinus terminology, duration, congestion, discharge, pressure, dental pain, fever, fatigue, cough, smell, headache, and assessed nasal or sinus fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines duration patterns and lists congestion, discharge, postnasal drip, dental discomfort, facial pain or pressure, fever, fatigue, cough, smell change, and headache.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped visual, swelling, severe headache, mental-status, fever, unilateral, and immunocompromise warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-selected imaging, return, escalation, and follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Limits imaging and other tests to clinician-selected contexts and lists emergency, specialist, systemic-illness, and non-response referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented symptom care, medicine decisions, questions, and management discussions without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Provides clinician-led conservative and pharmacological management choices; no dose-bearing content is mapped into this project.

unresolved_source_gaps: Throat and broad ear examination, prior-record review, fixed one-to-two-week timing, and diagnosis-specific medication details remain unsupported.
Sinusitis remains clinician-stated; individual purulence or facial pain must not be used alone to infer bacterial disease.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
